narrative.summary: Plan c2928492 added a `threads` subcommand to the lore engine plus a thin sibling `/workthreads` skill that reads the same SpecStory beats corpus and clusters related beats into work threads labeled new / open / closed. The plan ran in review mode with two tasks executed in order: task-0 (Coder, cli:claude-code) built the engine, fixtures, tests, and skill; task-1 (Reviewer, cli:codex) reviewed the result, wrote .deadreckon/REVIEW.md, and applied targeted fixes. Both tasks completed.

task_graph.title: task-0 -> task-1 dependency
task_graph.text: Two ordered tasks: task-0 (Coder, cli:claude-code) implements the feature with no dependencies; task-1 (Reviewer, cli:codex) depends on task-0 and reviews/fixes the completed implementation. task_order is [task-0, task-1].

task_graph.title: Review-mode orchestration
task_graph.text: The plan ran in mode=review: a coding lane followed by a fresh review/fix lane instructed to write .deadreckon/REVIEW.md first, then apply only fixes tied to findings and acceptance.

phases.title: Implementation (task-0)
phases.text: Coder lane (cli:claude-code, run c228b1f8, ~1133s wall) created lore/scripts/lib/threads.mjs, wired a threads subcommand into lore/scripts/mine-skills.mjs, added thread fixtures, tests, and the workthreads SKILL.md across two turns reported as complete and verified.

phases.title: Review and fix (task-1)
phases.text: Reviewer lane (cli:codex, run 1a6c8727, ~207s wall) wrote .deadreckon/REVIEW.md, then applied fixes tied to findings, notably adding command-gram reinforcement that the first implementation had omitted.

repairs.title: Command-gram reinforcement added in review
repairs.text: Review found the first implementation did not use the existing grams table for clustering reinforcement. The review-lane fix links shared command grams only when the gram contains at least one non-common, non-noise command head, satisfying the specified reinforcement signal while limiting accidental merges from generic test/git habits.

acceptance.title: dr-gate enforced acceptance
acceptance.text: Both lanes were gated by dr-gate against .deadreckon/acceptance.yaml. Acceptance artifacts present include no_em_dash.sh, threads_check.sh, and threads_classify.mjs under .deadreckon/acceptance.

acceptance.title: Full test suite must stay green
acceptance.text: The goal required `node --test tests/*.test.mjs` (existing engine tests plus the new threads.test.mjs) to pass; the threads tests assert multi-session merge into one thread, success-then-quiet => closed, recent-unresolved => open, and first-seen-in-window => new. Both runs reported all checks resolved, but no captured raw test-run output is present in the provided evidence.

open_threads.title: Fixture placement vs .specstory/history
open_threads.text: Open question carried in both decision docs: if committed fixtures must sit at a .specstory/history path under lore/fixtures, the two hardcoded count assertions in tests/engine.test.mjs would need updating. Left untouched to honor 'existing suite stays green'; confirmation requested on whether stricter fixture placement is desired.

as_built.system_overview: A new work-thread lens for the lore engine: lore/scripts/lib/threads.mjs clusters the existing beats corpus (SQLite via node:sqlite, sessions/beats/commands/grams tables) into work threads using union-find over shared signal tokens, assigns lifecycle status (closed/new/open) relative to an injectable clock, and emits a deterministic human digest (New / Open / Recently closed) plus optional --out file and --json output. A threads branch in lore/scripts/mine-skills.mjs wires it into the CLI, and lore/workthreads/SKILL.md exposes the /workthreads skill. No schema change, no PARSER_VERSION bump, no LLM/network call in the engine path.

components.title: Engine library (threads.mjs)
components.text: lore/scripts/lib/threads.mjs (+288) implements buildThreads: seeds beats carrying a touched file, executed command, or distinctive intent identifier; unions seeds sharing a link token (full file path, non-generic file-stem, identifier-shaped keyword, or distinctive command gram); assigns lifecycle with closed -> new -> open precedence using opts.nowMs (default Date.now()).

components.title: CLI wiring (mine-skills.mjs)
components.text: lore/scripts/mine-skills.mjs (+250/-241) gains --out and --json arg parsing and a threads subcommand branch; no other engine behavior changed.

components.title: Tests
components.text: lore/tests/threads.test.mjs (+140) runs under node --test, pinning a fixed clock anchor (2026-04-01) so committed fixtures classify identically, and asserts the four required lifecycle/merge behaviors. Identified as the load-bearing verification surface for the run.

components.title: Skill surface (workthreads SKILL.md)
components.text: lore/workthreads/SKILL.md (+143) is an agentskills.io-format skill named workthreads with description and allowed-tools, driving a lore-style guided start (Scope / Window / Goal) and calling the threads subcommand.

components.title: Thread fixtures
components.text: Five fixtures under lore/fixtures/threads/history/*.md encode threads with known lifecycle states (command-palette begin/finish, auth-token scaffold/fix, search-index start), indexed directly via --dir in the tests.

changed_files.text: New engine library clustering beats into work threads and labeling lifecycle (+288 / -0).
Added --out/--json flags and a threads subcommand branch (+250 / -241).
New node --test suite for the threads lens, anchored to a fixed clock (+140 / -0).
New /workthreads skill in agentskills.io format mirroring lore/SKILL.md (+143 / -0).
Fixture transcript: finish command-palette thread, completing a multi-session line of work (+42 / -0).
Run handoff notes regenerated with design-decisions and deviations sections (+72 / -4); appears in both runs' changed-file lists.

runtime_notes.title: Determinism
runtime_notes.text: The digest body embeds only corpus dates (never the wall clock) and sorts threads by a stable key (status precedence, then last/first activity desc, then id), so two runs on the same corpus are byte-identical.

runtime_notes.title: No engine schema or version change
runtime_notes.text: The engine path uses only existing corpus columns; no schema change, no PARSER_VERSION bump, and no LLM or network call. Capability constraints denied network and global install.

runtime_notes.title: Providers and spend
runtime_notes.text: task-0 ran on cli:claude-code (~1133s wall, subscription); task-1 ran on cli:codex (~207s wall, subscription). Both used sandbox=auto.

decisions.title: Clustering via union-find over shared signal tokens
decisions.text: Beats are seeded only when they carry a touched file, an executed command, or a distinctive intent identifier (reaction-only turns are skipped so they never spawn empty threads). Seeds union when sharing a link token: full file path, non-generic file-stem, identifier-shaped intent keyword (ALL_CAPS / snake-with-caps / CamelCase), or (after review) a distinctive command gram.

decisions.title: File-stem linking bridges intent to edit
decisions.text: The stem of src/ui/Palette.tsx is 'palette', so a session whose intent only names the palette links to the session that edits the file; this lets the gate's WIDGET_ALPHA thread merge even when the first session's touch path was never stored.

decisions.title: Lifecycle relative to an injectable clock
decisions.text: buildThreads accepts opts.nowMs (default Date.now()); the CLI uses the real clock while unit tests pin a fixed anchor (2026-04-01) so committed fixtures classify identically forever. Precedence is closed -> new -> open as specified.

tradeoffs.title: Conservative merging favors precision
tradeoffs.text: Linking only on files, identifier-shaped keywords, and distinctive command grams risks under-merging two sessions described in only generic prose with no shared file. The alternative (generic-noun linking) over-merges far more visibly, so the precision-favoring choice won.

tradeoffs.title: Singleton noise tolerated
tradeoffs.text: Beats with a lone distinctive identifier but no file can form singleton threads; they are bounded by the lifecycle windows (most are omitted) and cheap to skim, so no extra suppression heuristic was added.

deferrals.title: Stricter fixture placement deferred
deferrals.text: Committed thread fixtures live at lore/fixtures/threads/history/*.md with NO .specstory segment, because any .specstory/history under fixtures would be auto-discovered and break engine.test.mjs's hardcoded corpus counts (12 sessions/7 projects for --projects; 13 sessions/8 projects for --scan). They are indexed via --dir instead; moving them to a .specstory/history path is deferred pending confirmation.

deferrals.title: Generic content-word linking dropped
deferrals.text: An earlier version also linked threads on recurring common nouns; it over-merged the heterogeneous repo fixtures into one thread, so merging was reduced to files plus distinctive identifiers (plus reviewed command grams).

deferrals.title: Git repository initialized to run git-dependent checks
deferrals.text: The working directory arrived without a .git directory, so two acceptance checks that shell out to git (protected-file diff and no_em_dash.sh) exited 128/129. The lane ran git init and committed the tree as a baseline; protected files (lore/SKILL.md, lore/fixtures/golden/forge-plan.md) were never modified and diff clean.

children.summary: Coder lane (cli:claude-code, run c228b1f8): implemented lore/scripts/lib/threads.mjs, wired the threads subcommand with --out/--json into lore/scripts/mine-skills.mjs, added five thread fixtures, lore/tests/threads.test.mjs, and lore/workthreads/SKILL.md. Clustering via union-find on files/file-stems/identifier keywords; deterministic digest; no schema or PARSER_VERSION change. Reported complete and verified.

Reviewer lane (cli:codex, run 1a6c8727, depends on task-0): wrote .deadreckon/REVIEW.md, then applied targeted fixes, notably adding conservative command-gram reinforcement that the first implementation had omitted. Reported all checks resolved.
